claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK
circumstances: Claimant does not live in the UK
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'll help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment process.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Let me help you check your eligibility for Child Benefit. 

To get started, I need to know about the child (or children) you're claiming for:

**How old is your child?**

This is important because:
- Children **under 16** generally qualify automatically (with a few conditions)
- Children **aged 16-19** may qualify if they're in approved education or training
- Children **aged 20 or over** are not eligible for Child Benefit

Please tell me your child's age, and if you have multiple children, let me know about each of them.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'm interested in finding out if I can claim Child Benefit. I have one child, Alex, who is 8 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! Let me work through this with you.

So you have **Alex, who is 8 years old**. Since Alex is under 16, that's a good start.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to ask: **Is Alex working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work at all)?**

Given that Alex is only 8 years old, I would expect the answer to be yes (not in paid work), but I need to confirm this to continue the assessment.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't work at all - he's only 8! He's still in primary school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! That makes sense - Alex is in primary school and not working.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Next question: **Is Alex NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right?**

What I mean is - is Alex receiving any benefits like Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) or Universal Credit in his own name (not yours)?

For an 8-year-old in primary school, this would be very unusual, but I need to confirm.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't receive any benefits in his own name. He's just a regular 8-year-old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great, that's what I expected. Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Next: **Is Alex in any type of apprenticeship?**

Again, for an 8-year-old in primary school, the answer would typically be no, but I need to confirm this as part of the assessment.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in an apprenticeship - he's only 8 and in primary school!

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Absolutely right! Let me move forward with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now we're moving on to questions about **you** as the claimant. 

**Do you meet the UK residency requirements?**

You'll qualify if ANY of the following apply to you:

1. **You normally live in the UK** (this is the most common)
2. You have settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. You have pre-settled status and meet certain conditions (working, studying, or have sufficient resources)
4. You're living abroad as a Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

Do any of these apply to you? Most people simply answer "I normally live in the UK."

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Actually, I don't live in the UK. I'm living abroad at the moment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I see - you're living abroad. That's an important detail. Let me explore this further.

There are some situations where you can still claim Child Benefit while living abroad. Can you tell me more about your situation?

**Do any of these apply to you:**

1. **Are you a Crown servant** (working for the UK government abroad, such as in the diplomatic service or armed forces)?

2. **Are you living in certain qualifying countries** with a connection to the UK (such as Gibraltar, or certain countries where Crown servants' family members reside)?

3. Do you have any connection to the **EU Settlement Scheme** (settled or pre-settled status)?

If none of these apply, could you tell me:
- Which country are you currently living in?
- Do you have any ongoing connection to the UK (like you're temporarily abroad but still considered a UK resident)?

This will help me determine if you meet the residency requirements.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, none of those apply to me. I'm just living abroad - not a Crown servant, not in Gibraltar or anything like that, and no EU Settlement Scheme status. I'm not temporarily away either - I'm actually living abroad now.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for clarifying that. Unfortunately, this is going to affect your eligibility.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Assessment Result: **NOT ELIGIBLE**

I'm sorry to inform you that **you are not eligible for Child Benefit** for Alex.

### Why you're not eligible:

While Alex meets all the child-related requirements (he's 8 years old, in primary school, not working, not receiving benefits in his own name, and not in an apprenticeship), **you do not meet the UK residency requirements** which are mandatory for claiming Child Benefit.

To claim Child Benefit, you must either:
- Normally live in the UK, OR
- Meet special circumstances such as:
  - Having settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme (with qualifying criteria)
  - Being a Crown servant working abroad
  - Living in certain qualifying countries like Gibraltar

Since you're living abroad permanently and none of the special circumstances apply to you, unfortunately Child Benefit is not available.

### What this means:

Child Benefit is a UK benefit designed for residents of the UK. Even though Alex would otherwise qualify, the residency requirement applies to the person claiming (you), not just the child.

If your circumstances change and you return to live in the UK, you would be able to claim Child Benefit at that time.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about this decision?

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old and meets the child-related qualifying criteria: under 16, not working, not receiving benefits in their own right, and not in an apprenticeship. However, the claimant does not meet the UK residency requirements. The claimant is living abroad and does not qualify under any of the special circumstances (not a Crown servant, not living in qualifying countries, no EU Settlement Scheme status, and not temporarily away with ongoing UK residency). Child Benefit requires the claimant to normally live in the UK or meet one of the special residency exceptions. Therefore, the claim for Alex is ineligible due to the claimant's residency status.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (age 8), who would otherwise qualify as a child under 16. However, the claimant does not meet the mandatory UK residency requirements for Child Benefit. The claimant is living abroad permanently and does not qualify under any of the special circumstances that would allow claiming while abroad. Child Benefit is therefore not available.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.reason: Does not meet UK residency requirements
response.guidance: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances (settled status, pre-settled status with qualifying criteria, Crown servant, or living in certain countries). If you have pre-settled status, you may need to be working, studying, or have sufficient resources.

However, the claimant does not meet the UK residency requirements. The claimant is living abroad and does not qualify under any of the special circumstances (not a Crown servant, not living in qualifying countries, no EU Settlement Scheme status, and not temporarily away with ongoing UK residency). Child Benefit requires the claimant to normally live in the UK or meet one of the special residency exceptions. Therefore, the claim for Alex is ineligible due to the claimant's residency status.

However, the claimant does not meet the mandatory UK residency requirements for Child Benefit. The claimant is living abroad permanently and does not qualify under any of the special circumstances that would allow claiming while abroad. Child Benefit is therefore not available.
